content_markdown:
A voltmeter is used to measure the potential difference, $V$, across one of four identical filament light bulbs that are connected in series to a $12.0\text{ V}$ battery as shown in the diagram below. The reading on the voltmeter is $3.0\text{ V}$.

If the filament of the bulb being measured **breaks**, the reading on the voltmeter will be

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a 12V battery connected in series to four identical light bulbs. A voltmeter is connected in parallel across the fourth bulb.

vibe_explanation: When the filament of the bulb breaks, the circuit becomes open, meaning no current flows through the other bulbs. However, the voltmeter has a very high resistance. When it is connected across the break, it effectively becomes the only component in series with the battery (the other bulbs now act as simple wires with negligible resistance compared to the voltmeter). Therefore, the voltmeter will measure the full electromotive force (emf) of the battery.
